ai_overview: Viktor's Stakeholder Digest email was not available for 07/05/2026 (Microsoft 365 authentication pending) — this digest is built from Slack and Jira sources only. Thursday 7 May was a productive release day: Chloe Christie coordinated 14 tickets across BAU and high-priority streams for the main weekly release, confirmed clear by Robin John's QA sign-off on the morning of 8 May. Ciara Carroll was the standout contributor, completing three Jira tickets (GDCU-8956 Claude Design Image Project Assistant, GDCU-8736 Learning Hub Imagery, GDCU-8947 Cosmo Cash Machine Images) while also publishing the new Claude project graphics assistant to #gdc-product-design-pod and #gdc-nonen-sync. Jessica Dordevic completed GDCU-8879 (Genesis Brand Carousel Component) and GDCU-8928 (IUJ Banners) and is deeply engaged on the sports quiz hub prototype via active DMs with Mike. Novica Vukobratovic shipped GDCU-8877 (Phone Numbers on Email Confirmation Pages Admin Panel), which was included in the release. Harden Wallace has GDCU-8955 (Cool Tones Phase 2) In QA and GDCU-8874 (Page Bloat SEO) In Product. Colin Brannigan chaired Matrix API dependency resolution across division leads in #core-division-leads and shared a Claude Code industry briefing late evening. Dean Ryan raised concerns in DMs about Viktor-generated analytics reports containing hallucinated user-drop-off data. Vic Dadson remains on medical sick leave, now confirmed extended until 16 May (doctor's note uploaded to Bob). 18 of 22 tracked team members showed some level of activity on 7 May.

people.name: Vic Dadson
people.summary: Remains on medical sick leave, now confirmed signed off until 16 May — doctor's note uploaded directly to Bob without notifying Mike or HR. Mike informed Colin of the extension via DM on 6 May. No Slack or Jira activity on 7 May. On return, Mike has flagged a conversation with Nemanja and the data team for knowledge-sharing and support.

people.name: Jessica Dordevic
people.summary: Highly productive on 7 May — completed GDCU-8879 (Genesis Brand Carousel Component Card) and GDCU-8928 (IUJ Banners), with GDCU-8948 (Update Ratio of Prize Boxes) moved to In Product. Also active on the sports quiz hub prototype: sent Mike a working prototype link (jessica-projects.netlify.app/gdcu-8903/quiz-hub), then received detailed design feedback from Mike on 6 May covering sports scope (Football, Basketball, American Football, Tennis to start), scoring explanation copy, removing AI artefacts (em-dashes), and signed-in vs signed-out variant requirements. Reviewed Harden's Cool Tones comments on GDCU-8834 in #gdc-product-design-pod (6 May). Note: messaged Mike on the morning of 8 May requesting a leave day due to personal circumstances.

people.name: Miguel Migneco
people.summary: On 6 May received the World Cup Predictor game prototype link from Mike (world-cup-predictor-alpha.vercel.app) for review ahead of Mike's return from leave. GDCU-8935 (World Cup Predictor Game UX) remains in Backlog pending design work. No active channel contributions or Jira ticket updates visible on 7 May.

people.name: Ciara Carroll
people.summary: Most active contributor on 7 May — completed three Jira tickets: GDCU-8956 (Claude Design Image Graphic Project Assistant), GDCU-8736 (Learning Hub Imagery), and GDCU-8947 (Cosmo Cash Machine Images). GDCU-8619 (Operator List Redesign) remains In Progress. Published the new Claude project graphics assistant in both #gdc-product-design-pod and #gdc-nonen-sync, sharing updated documentation (incl. Genesis media manager guidance replacing Origins) and the live Claude project link trained and tested on 6HPC image banners. Also raised a Norwegian translation verification query in #gdc-nonen-sync. GDCU-8846 (Spring Ad Creative for Cosmo Spins) was cancelled.

people.name: Josh Smith
people.summary: No direct Slack or Jira activity visible on 7 May. On 6 May participated in the weekly product sync (Chloe had circulated the agenda on 5 May). Most recent channel contribution was 1 May in #gdc-nonen-sync announcing the new Card Carousel shortcode with Confluence docs. Coordinating DC9 sprint planning and the Carousel Banner Shortcode design decision raised in #gdc-product-design-pod on 1 May.

people.name: Chloe Christie
people.summary: Highly active release coordinator on 7 May. In #6amigos posted the full upcoming release list (9 BAU tickets + 5 high-priority including GDCU-8842, GDCU-8900, GDCU-8861, GDCU-8865, GDCU-8849) and asked Eoin to review GDCU-8839 for dev concerns. In #gdc-pod raised two site issues: duplicate payment methods appearing on Iceland English brand reviews (is/en), and an H2 tag in the sign-up modal potentially affecting SEO across all geos (ticket may be needed). Active DM exchanges with Mike (5 May) aligning on World Cup Predictor and Group Bets delivery timeline — June target confirmed, with a KO call flagged as needed once design is complete.

people.name: Carlos Blauth
people.summary: Completed GDCU-8733 (Apply Feedback to the Learning Hub) on 7 May. No Slack channel or DM activity visible on 7 May. Remains a member of the #gdc-f2p-apps and #gdc-deep-link-betslip-project channels for ongoing work on the FTP app and Ace embedded betslip.

people.name: Eoin McCarthy
people.summary: In #6amigos on 7 May confirmed no concerns with GDCU-8839 (AB Testing for UK Tenancy Removal) for Chloe — 'Looks good to me 👍'. Minimal other Slack or Jira activity on 7 May. Last DM with Mike was 22 April re: Betting Tips Hub pagination feasibility.

people.name: Harden Wallace
people.summary: Two active Jira tickets on 7 May — GDCU-8955 (Cool Tones Phase 2 UX/Dev fixes) is In QA awaiting sign-off, and GDCU-8874 (Page Bloat SEO — reducing HTML payload size across all markets including CookieYes, SSR operator list, VWO, inline scripts) has moved to In Product. Chloe asked Jessica to review Harden's detailed comments on GDCU-8834 in #gdc-product-design-pod (6 May), with a partial release planned shortly and remaining fixes the following week. No direct Slack messages from Harden on 7 May.

people.name: Kelvin Yee
people.summary: No Slack or Jira activity on 7 May. Last DM with Mike was 9 April — discussed filtering stale racing tips from the News Hub Most Popular section using Site Article Categories + publish timestamp logic. No tickets updated in the Jira scan.

people.name: Milan Nikolic
people.summary: No Slack or Jira activity on 7 May. Last DM with Mike was 26 March regarding Global Strategy Hub (GDCU-8653) localisation approach — agreed to serve page in English with localised casino results as v1. No activity visible across April or May 2026.

people.name: Nebojsa Stanic
people.summary: GDCU-8847 (Display RevShare Operator List to VIP Customers tagged in Ktag) is In QA on 7 May — the ticket was updated on 7 May at 15:17 BST. No Slack activity visible on 7 May. Last DM with Mike was December 2025 (Tinder-style casino card prototype demo).

people.name: Novica Vukobratovic
people.summary: Completed GDCU-8877 (Add Field to Manage Phone Numbers on Email Confirmation Form Pages in Admin Panel) on 7 May — a feature enabling a toggleable/mandatory phone number field on email landing pages (/e and /p paths) managed per-URL via the admin panel. This ticket was included in Chloe's release list for the main weekly push. No Slack activity visible on 7 May.

people.name: Sanja Budic
people.summary: On 5 May chased Mike via DM about the /picks-with-odds Matrix API endpoint she had requested the previous week, confirming she had tagged him in the relevant Slack thread. Active on API endpoint integration work for the Ace AI feature suite. No Jira tickets updated on 7 May.

people.name: Robin John
people.summary: Completed QA verification of the full 7 May weekly release — posted confirmation to #gdc-pod on the morning of 8 May: 'QA have verified the tickets which went live yesterday and everything looks fine.' Active QA coverage across all DC9 release tickets. No DM history with Mike visible.

people.name: Shane McGrath
people.summary: On 6 May Gabriel directed Shane in #gdc-pod on how to test the Cosmo Spins idle tab suspension issue (open game, open new tab, allow suspension via browser memory management, then re-open). No direct Slack or Jira activity visible on 7 May. Supporting Robin on the May 7 release QA alongside ongoing issue investigations.

people.name: Dean Ryan
people.summary: Active DM exchange with Mike on 6 May raising concerns about AI-generated analytics report accuracy — a Viktor-generated report suggested a 20k user drop-off but the referenced URLs indicated only ~9k, flagging a hallucination issue. Expressed frustration about the risks of accepting AI output uncritically ('I hate what people can do with AI now and cause complete carnage'). Also noted that 12k of the FTP app's user base are Indonesian, flagging a need for commercial partners in that market. Was in #core-division-leads on 7 May querying what the team is waiting on from Matrix.

people.name: Michael Butlin
people.summary: Brief DM exchange with Mike on 5 May — light conversation around project delivery frustrations (Mike noting pushback/descoping of key features). Michael had been working until 2am on 30 April on WC Predictor quiz copy. No meaningful Slack channel or Jira activity on 7 May.

people.name: Dan Smullen
people.summary: No Slack or Jira activity on 7 May. Last active 28 April in DMs with Mike navigating Claude Design access (locating the canvas via the easel icon). Previously flagged F2P game issues in #gdc-f2p-apps in April.

people.name: Gabriel Cornoiu
people.summary: Active in #core-division-leads on 7 May — shared the Matrix OddsFeeds API usage Confluence page in response to Colin's request for all pending Matrix requests by COB, pointing to the 'Pending Requests' section. Also in #gdc-pod raised the duplicate payment methods issue on Iceland English brand reviews (is/en) — likely a language vs. locale filtering bug. Brief DM with Mike on 6 May. Earlier in the week noted in #6amigos that Genesis would release the Brands Carousel component Thursday morning just before the weekly release.

people.name: Diana Thurmann
people.summary: Limited direct activity on 7 May. In #gdc-nonen-sync Ciara's Claude project assistant announcement is relevant to Diana's non-EN market remit. In #core-division-leads Lynsey shared a Sports Odds Data Feed integration wishlist Confluence page, which intersects with Diana's non-EN markets scope. Diana's automated #gdc-nonen-market-updates channel continues publishing daily industry updates. Last direct Slack post visible was 1 May in #gdc-nonen-sync.

people.name: Colin Brannigan
people.summary: Highly active on 7 May — in #core-division-leads led Matrix API dependency resolution, requesting all pending Matrix requests from division leads by COB, questioning what was outstanding from Matrix, and coordinating with Gabriel and Dean. Was in the office. Late evening (22:07) shared a detailed CNBC Claude Code interview summary to #core-division-leads (Boris Cherny, Head of Claude Code at Anthropic) covering AI-driven engineering productivity, the future of the 'Software Engineer' title, and the shift from execution to ideation as the bottleneck. Via DM received update from Mike on 6 May about Vic Dadson's sick leave extension to 16 May. Earlier in the week sent Mike the Q1 overview context and discussed NZ regulatory risk.
